name: Azagadores Trail (PR-MU 92)
name_original: Trail run hasta el Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras (PR-MU 92)
description: Run the 7.8-mile (12.5 km) Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras (PR-MU 92) trail, a moderate route through lunar-like landscapes to turquoise w
region_intro: Jogging around Alhama De Murcia offers diverse terrain, from mountain ranges to unique badlands. The region is characterized by the Sierra de Espuña and Sierra de la Muela mountain ranges, providing varied elevation and natural landscapes. Runners can explore trails through lush forests, along serene paths, and around the Algeciras Reservoir. The distinctive Barrancos de Gebas, with its "lunar-like" landscape, also presents visually striking running experiences. Best jogging routes around Alhama De Murcia The most popular jogging route is Running circular through the Sierra de La Muela to the Algeciras Reservoir viewpoint, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers significant elevation changes and views of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Azagadores Trail (PR-MU 92), a moderate 7.8 miles (12.5 km) path. This route traverses the unique Barrancos de Gebas, known for its eroded formations and distinctive landscape. Local runners also love the Siete Hermanos Trail, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Espuña Regional Park, often completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes. Jogging around Alhama De Murcia is defined by mountain ranges, badlands, and reservoirs, offering a rich tapestry of natural features. The network provides options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Alhama De Murcia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Alhama De Murcia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alhama De Murcia?
A: Alhama De Murcia offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 80 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy jogs to challenging mountain runs.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Alhama De Murcia?
A: The region boasts diverse terrain, from the lush forests and serene paths of the Sierra de Espuña Regional Park to the unique 'lunar-like' landscapes of the Barrancos de Gebas. You'll also find routes around the Algeciras Reservoir and through the Sierra de la Muela and Sierra de Carrascoy mountain ranges, offering varied elevation and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Alhama De Murcia?
A: Yes, Alhama De Murcia has several easy running options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6892040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Jardin 13&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.7-mile (5.9 km) route with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed jog. Overall, there are 4 easy routes available in the area.

Q: What are some challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Alhama De Murcia offers 39 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900792147/running-circular-through-the-sierra-de-la-muela-to-the-algeciras-reservoir-viewpoint" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular through the Sierra de La Muela to the Algeciras Reservoir viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 9.2-mile (14.8 km) trail with significant elevation changes and stunning views.

Q: Can I find running routes with unique natural features or landmarks?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural monuments. You can explore the distinctive eroded formations of the Barrancos de Gebas on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900780595/azagadores-trail-pr-mu-92" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azagadores Trail (PR-MU 92)&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de Algeciras Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5650751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de la Arboleja gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and various summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running trails that offer views of reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, the Algeciras Reservoir is a prominent feature in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900792147/running-circular-through-the-sierra-de-la-muela-to-the-algeciras-reservoir-viewpoint" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular through the Sierra de La Muela to the Algeciras Reservoir viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers excellent vistas of this water body. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de Algeciras Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; as a highlight along some routes.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Alhama De Murcia?
A: The running routes in Alhama De Murcia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from mountain ranges to unique badlands, and the extensive network of trails suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in the area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, many of the easier and moderate routes, particularly those with less elevation gain and well-maintained paths, can be enjoyed by families. Consider routes within the Sierra de Espuña Regional Park for accessible options.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Alhama De Murcia?
A: Alhama De Murcia benefits from a climate conducive to year-round exercise. However, spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for running, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, days of winter. Early mornings or late afternoons are generally best during warmer months.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Alhama De Murcia are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900792147/running-circular-through-the-sierra-de-la-muela-to-the-algeciras-reservoir-viewpoint" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular through the Sierra de La Muela to the Algeciras Reservoir viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region's diverse trail system includes numerous loop options for varied experiences.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes offer glimpses of historical interest. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17268931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Jumilla – Castle of Alhama de Murcia loop from Alhama de Murcia&lt;/a&gt; is a 12-mile (19.3 km) route that includes views of the Castle of Alhama de Murcia, blending natural beauty with local heritage.

Q: Are there dedicated sports facilities for runners in Alhama De Murcia?
A: Beyond the natural trails, Alhama de Murcia also provides public sports facilities. The Complejo Deportivo Guadalentín, for instance, includes a running track, catering to those who prefer track running or specific training.
tips: The Rambla de Algeciras Reservoir is located in the municipality of Alhama de Murcia, in the region of Murcia. It was built as part of the General Flood Defense Plan for the Segura River Basin. The reservoir has several purposes, including flood control, water storage, and regulating the flow of water for irrigation.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail run?
faqs.answer: The Trail run to the Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras (PR-MU 92) is approximately 12.5 km long. For jogging, it typically takes around 1 hour and 22 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on individual fitness and pace.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While some sections are easily accessible, the route is generally considered an intermediate run due to its length, elevation gain (around 249m), and potentially irregular terrain in the middle section. Good fitness is recommended, but it can be enjoyed by various skill levels if approached cautiously.

faqs.question: Is the PR-MU 92 a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Trail run to the Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras (PR-MU 92) is generally a looped trail, offering a circular route back to your starting point.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in the Murcia region of Spain. While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, it's advisable to look for parking areas near the Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras or the nearby town of Gebas. There are no shuttles mentioned, so personal transport is likely needed.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. While some paths are easily accessible, the middle section can be irregular and uneven. The trail passes through the dramatic badlands of the 'Ravines of Gebas,' which feature unique geological formations resembling a desert or lunar surface. It's important to note that the trail can become impassable during periods of heavy rain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during cooler months. It's strongly advised against completing the route during extreme heat without adequate protection, and heavy rain can make sections impassable. Sunny days are ideal for experiencing the dramatic scenery and shadows.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect truly unique and stunning scenery. The route traverses the 'Ravines of Gebas,' a Protected Landscape with 'otherworldly' badlands and bizarrely shaped hills. Upon reaching the Rambla de Algeciras Reservoir, you'll be greeted by tranquil turquoise waters and breathtaking panoramic views of the reservoir and distant mountains.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this area?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the 'Ravines of Gebas,' which is a Protected Landscape. However, there are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for accessing the trail itself. It's always a good idea to check local regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Trail run to the Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras (PR-MU 92) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected natural areas, and to be mindful of other trail users and potential wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there facilities or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no facilities or water sources directly on the trail. Visitors should bring their own food and sufficient water for the duration of their run.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route follows the Sendero de los Azagadores for its entire length. It also intersects with sections of other trails, including Esputrek. Tramo 6 Gebas - El Berro and Espubike. Tramo 2 Gebas - Casas Nuevas.
